growth_records.strain: VPI-5482 (wild type and thiamine-biosynthesis/transport deletion mutants)
growth_records.medium: modified minimal medium (after Varel and Bryant; Scholle et al.)
growth_records.cite: Costliow et al. Thiamine Acquisition Strategies Impact Metabolism and Competition in the Gut Microbe Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron. mSystems (2017). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00116-17. [PMC5613172]
growth_records.snippet: It is important to note that growth defects only affected the terminal density of the mutants, and the doubling times remained fairly constant (~2 h).
growth_records.notes: Text explicitly states doubling times of B. thetaiotaomicron VPI-5482 wild-type and thiamine acquisition/biosynthesis mutants (grown in the modified minimal medium across 0-10,000 nM thiamine) remained fairly constant at ~2 h; only terminal density (yield) was affected by genotype/thiamine level, not doubling time. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/2 h = 0.347 h^-1 (rounded 0.35) from the approximate (~2 h) doubling time given in text. Routine anaerobic culturing at 37C per Materials and Methods (though the specific thiamine growth-assay temperature was not restated, 37C anaerobic chamber conditions are the paper's standard culturing condition for this organism).

growth_records.medium: Wilkins-Chalgren (WC) Anaerobe Broth (Oxoid)

up.method: Table 1: consumes glucose, pyruvate, mannose (rates modeled, not tabulated numerically)

sec.method: Table 1: produces acetate, lactate, succinate, formate (no numeric flux reported)

growth_records.cite: Garza et al. Emergence of alternative states in a synthetic human gut microbial community. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-67036-5. [PMC12789478]
growth_records.snippet: the medium ... was delivered at an approximate rate of 0.04 h−1, resulting in a complete change of the medium in 24 hours. ... Control samples converge to a steady state in which Blautia hydrogenotrophica, Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron, and
growth_records.notes: Synthetic gut community in emulated chemostat, feed/dilution rate ~0.04 h-1 (24 h turnover). At chemostat steady state coexisting species grow at mu=D, so mu=0.04 h-1 (calculated). doubling_time=ln2/0.04=17.3 h. WC broth is a commercial Oxoid product; composition not enumerated in text (medium_composition_stated=false); only trehalose 0.71 mM measured, plus glucose+pyruvate present. Metabolite uptake/secretion given only qualitatively (Table 1: B. thetaiotaomicron glucose/pyruvate/mannose modes consume glucose,pyruvate,mannose and produce acetate,lactate,succinate,formate); no numeric fluxes. Temperature/pH not stated in extractable text (pH is an emergent controlled variable 5.0-6.5 in simulations).

growth_records.medium: Wilkins-Chalgren Anaerobe Broth (WC; Oxoid)

up.method: reported as main carbon source, consumed/depleted by ~20-24 h; concentration time-course in Fig. 2a only, no numeric rate given
reported as main carbon source, consumed/depleted; Fig. 2a only, no numeric rate

sec.method: identified fermentation product (HPLC), Fig. 2a; no numeric rate in text

growth_records.cite: Liu et al. Starvation responses impact interaction dynamics of human gut bacteria Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron and Roseburia intestinalis. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01501-1. [PMC10579405]
growth_records.snippet: When grown in monoculture, B. thetaiotaomicron produced succinate, lactate, formate, and acetate during fermentation, which decreased the pH from 6.7 to 5.0. At 20 h, B. thetaiotaomicron reached its maximal density of 1.2 x 10^6 viable cell
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic monoculture in WC (no mucin). Growth reported as viable cell counts by flow cytometry (live/dead SYBR Green/PI), NOT OD or gDW; max 1.2e6 viable cells/ul at 20 h; density then declined after glucose/pyruvate depletion due to pH drop 6.7->5.0. No specific growth rate (mu) or doubling time reported; cannot be computed because initial viable cell density (cells/ul) at t=0 is not stated (inoculum only given as 1 ml of OD600 0.1 preculture into 60 ml). qS/qP not computable: substrate and product concentrations are shown only in Fig. 2a with no tabulated numeric values, and biomass is in cells/ul not gDW. Products succinate/lactate/formate/acetate are HPLC-identified; propionate not detected (medium lacks vitamin B12). WC (Oxoid) full composition not stated in paper; only glucose and pyruvate named as main carbon sources. temperature 37 C, 170 rpm stirring, initial pH 6.7.

growth_records.strain: B. thetaiotaomicron VPI-5482 (DSM 2079T) + R. intestinalis L1-82 (DSM 14610T)
growth_records.medium: Wilkins-Chalgren Anaerobe Broth (WC; Oxoid)

sec.method: net lactate significantly lower than in either monoculture (p<0.001); Fig. 2c, qualitative

growth_records.cite: Liu et al. Starvation responses impact interaction dynamics of human gut bacteria Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron and Roseburia intestinalis. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01501-1. [PMC10579405]
growth_records.snippet: For the co-culture grown in WC, the co-utilization of glucose and pyruvate resulted in two separate density peaks of 7.4 and 4.6 x 10^5 viable cells/ul for B. thetaiotaomicron and R. intestinalis, respectively... The pH decreased from 6.7 t
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic two-species co-culture in WC (no mucin), species-resolved by flow cytometry + UMAP/KNN classification. Peak viable densities: BT 7.4e5 cells/ul, RI 4.6e5 cells/ul; RI second peak 4.1e5 at 38 h. pH 6.7->5.3 (less acidic than BT mono 5.0, more than RI mono 5.7). Net lactate lower than either monoculture. No mu/doubling time; not computable (cells/ul, no t=0 density, figure-only substrate/product data). WC composition not stated; carbon sources glucose+pyruvate. 37 C, 170 rpm.

growth_records.medium: Wilkins-Chalgren Anaerobe Broth (WC; Oxoid) with porcine mucin beads

up.method: foraged after glucose depletion; RNA-seq up-regulation of galactosidase BT_3340/BT_4684, fucosidase BT_1842; qualitative

sec.method: net increase of 0.12 mM after 120 h (significant, p<0.001) from mucin hydrolysis; released not consumed

growth_records.cite: Liu et al. Starvation responses impact interaction dynamics of human gut bacteria Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron and Roseburia intestinalis. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01501-1. [PMC10579405]
growth_records.snippet: After depleting glucose and pyruvate at 24 h, planktonic B. thetaiotaomicron retained a viable cell count higher than 1.0 x 10^6 cells/ul (Fig. 2d)... Attached B. thetaiotaomicron ... reached maximal densities of 6.0 x 10^5 viable cells/ul
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic BT monoculture in WC + mucin beads. Planktonic viable cells stayed >1.0e6 cells/ul after glucose/pyruvate depletion (24 h) and through 288 h (long-term survival via mucin foraging). Bead-attached cells max 6.0e5 viable cells/ul at 24 h. Net Neu5Ac release +0.12 mM over 120 h (only explicitly numeric metabolite change in text; this is a released mucin-degradation product, sign = secretion). No mu/doubling time reported/computable (cells/ul, no t=0 density). Mucin bead composition (per 100 ml gel) IS stated and listed; WC broth base composition not stated, hence medium_composition_stated=false for the growth medium overall. 37 C, 170 rpm, initial pH 6.7.

growth_records.strain: B. thetaiotaomicron VPI-5482 (DSM 2079T) + R. intestinalis L1-82 (DSM 14610T)
growth_records.medium: Wilkins-Chalgren Anaerobe Broth (WC; Oxoid) with porcine mucin beads

up.method: both species hydrolyze mucin; galactose significantly higher than BT monoculture; qualitative

sec.method: net release from mucin hydrolysis significantly higher than BT monoculture (p<0.001), Fig. 2f; released

growth_records.cite: Liu et al. Starvation responses impact interaction dynamics of human gut bacteria Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron and Roseburia intestinalis. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01501-1. [PMC10579405]
growth_records.snippet: For the co-culture grown in the presence of mucin beads, B. thetaiotaomicron reached a two-fold higher maximal density than without beads (1.5 x 10^6 viable cells/ul at 16 h). In contrast, R. intestinalis ... lower maximal density of 1.8 x
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic BT+RI co-culture in WC + mucin beads, species-resolved by flow cytometry. BT peak 1.5e6 viable cells/ul at 16 h (2-fold higher than without beads); RI peak 1.8e5 viable cells/ul (lower than WC co-culture, mucin suppresses RI). pH 6.7->5.1. RI showed a second peak at 120 h (~10-fold rise 96->120 h). Net galactose release significantly higher than BT monoculture (both species hydrolyze mucin). No mu/doubling time reported/computable (cells/ul, no t=0 density; figure-only fluxes). Mucin bead composition stated; WC base composition not stated. 37 C, 170 rpm, initial pH 6.7.

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic minimal medium with dextran (CSTR feed)
growth_records.cite: Khazaei et al. Metabolic multistability and hysteresis in a model aerobe-anaerobe microbiome community. Science Advances (2020). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.aba0353. [PMC7423363]
growth_records.snippet: a residence time of 5 hours (input and output flow rates of 40 ml/hour) was used for all experiments... In Kp-Bt state conditions (5, 2, 1, 0.25, and 0 mM glucose), where Bt growth persisted... three samples separated by at least one reside
growth_records.notes: CSTR steady state; D = 40 ml/h / 200 ml = 0.2 h-1. Bt persists (does not wash out) in the Kp-Bt state, therefore at steady state mu_Bt = D = 0.2 h-1; doubling time = ln2/0.2 = 3.47 h. Bt breaks down dextran to oligosaccharides/glucose and produces short-chain fatty acids (acetate, lactate) qualitatively, but no experimental qP/qS values are tabulated in text. Strain VPI-5482 is the genome-scale model strain (iAH991); experimental Bt strain not separately named.

growth_records.medium: Carbon-source minimal medium (batch, dextran)

up.units: vmax fitted (Monod), value in table S1
up.method: batch OD600 kinetics, Bayesian MCMC fit

growth_records.cite: Khazaei et al. Metabolic multistability and hysteresis in a model aerobe-anaerobe microbiome community. Science Advances (2020). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.aba0353. [PMC7423363]
growth_records.snippet: Overnight cultures grown anaerobically in minimal medium supplemented with either 0.5% (w/v) dextran... diluted 1:20 (for Bt)... resuspended at optical density (OD) 1 (for Bt)... The plate was incubated at 37°C, and OD 600 was measured ever
growth_records.notes: Batch mu_max and Monod vmax/Km for Bt on dextran were fitted but numeric values are only in table S1 / fig. S3 (supplementary), not in fulltext; growth_rate and uptake left null. No in-text numbers to compute a rate.

growth_records.cite: Catlett JL et al. Metabolic Feedback Inhibition Influences Metabolite Secretion by the Human Gut Symbiont Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00252-20. [PMC7470985]
growth_records.snippet: 0 mM ... Doubling time (h) 1.322 ... SD 0.047 (Table 1). Cultures grown under strict anaerobic conditions at 37C ... glucose to 0.05% (wt/vol) (2.78 mM) under a 5% H2, 20% CO2, N2 atmosphere.
growth_records.notes: Control condition, no acetate/formate added. Doubling time 1.322 h reported (Table 1, n=6 biological replicates). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/1.322 = 0.524 /h. Medium: defined minimal, glucose 0.05% wt/vol = 2.78 mM, buffered pH 7.2 with 1 M potassium phosphate; full 25-component composition only in supplement. Secreted metabolites (acetate, succinate, formate, propionate, small lactate, plus His/Cys/cystine/glutathione/Asn/Ala) reported only as spent-medium concentrations (mM, in figures) and as percent mole-carbon fluxes (Fig 2; CO2 inferred 4.6%), NOT as per-biomass rates, so no numeric mmol/gDW/h uptake/secretion rates extractable from text. FBA max-uptake settings (glucose 2.78, acetate/formate 10 mmol/gDW/h) are MODEL INPUTS, not measurements.

growth_records.medium: Defined medium M20 (20 amino acids), phosphate-buffered; grouped substrates fructose/sucrose/levan/FOSmix at 5 g/L

sec.units: mmol/gDW (produced; 0, 0.6, 1.3 over intervals; paper convention negative=production)
mmol/gDW (approx, small amounts in some cases)

mfa.note: vs total amino acid consumption 12.4 mmol/gDW in M20; full 79 independent / 240 dependent flux network solved (Data Sheet S1)
exceeds biomass protein requirement (~5.0 mmol/gDW) ~2-4x; surplus routed to energy/redox balancing

growth_records.cite: Adamberg, Signe et al. Degradation of Fructans and Production of Propionic Acid by Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron are Enhanced by the Shortage of Amino Acids. Frontiers in Nutrition (2014). DOI:10.3389/fnut.2014.00021. [PMC4428435]
growth_records.snippet: The average growth rates (mu, 1/h) during the first (fast) growth phase in the M20 medium were 1.02 +/- 0.11 h-1 and in the M2 medium 0.82 +/- 0.18 ... The average biomass yields per substrate consumed (YXS) ... were 0.04 +/- 0.01 gDW/g-hex
growth_records.notes: Doubling_time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/1.02 = 0.68 h (M20). mu reported (calorimetric power-time curve, first fast phase, grouped fructose/sucrose/levan/FOSmix). Table 2 values are specific amounts in mmol/gDW accumulated per interval (0-24, 24-48, 48-168 h), NOT per-hour rates; totals = sum of intervals (e.g., acetate 83+52+30=165). Paper table footnote: 'Negative values for amino acids and gases indicate the production' (so positive AA = consumption; Ala/Glu shown as produced). NOTE contradiction: text states H2 and CO2 were PRODUCED during first 48 h yet table lists them positive; I report CO2 36.9 and H2 6.6 mmol/gDW as net production per the text, direction flagged uncertain. Biomass from heat via 20 kJ/gDW. Final pH 5.6+/-0.2 (M20). No growth on long-chain inulin HP as sole carbohydrate or in carbohydrate-free medium. Biomass composition for MFA taken from E. coli at mu=0.35 h-1 (no Bacteroides data). 75% of levan consumed and 90% of final biomass formed by 48 h.

growth_records.medium: Defined amino-acid-restricted medium M2 (Cys-HCl + His only), phosphate-buffered; grouped substrates fructose/sucrose/levan/FOSmix at 5 g/L

up.compound: L-glutamate (net; produced 24-48 h then consumed 48-168 h)

sec.units: mmol/gDW (total; 0.08+/-0.1, 64+/-38, 53+/-51); almost no lactate in first 24 h
mmol/gDW (total; 18+/-12, 90+/-31, 128+/-86); enhanced under amino acid deficiency
mmol/gDW (net produced; 2.0 produced 24-48 h, 0.2 consumed 48-168 h); ~3x higher production than in M20

mfa.note: amino-acid deficiency shifts product spectrum toward propionate via increased acetyl-CoA and anaplerotic oxaloacetate/reductive TCA flux; less D-lactate

mfa.units: mmol/gDW (48-168 h interval; net Sum-AA 0, -7.6 produced, 14.4 consumed)
mfa.note: B. thetaiotaomicron synthesizes required amino acids from ammonium sulfate; Ala/Glu produced for NAD+ regeneration

growth_records.cite: Adamberg, Signe et al. Degradation of Fructans and Production of Propionic Acid by Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron are Enhanced by the Shortage of Amino Acids. Frontiers in Nutrition (2014). DOI:10.3389/fnut.2014.00021. [PMC4428435]
growth_records.snippet: in the M2 medium 0.82 +/- 0.18 ... A shortage of amino acids was shown to stimulate the production of propionic acid by B. thetaiotaomicron ... increased consumption of levan (69 vs 84%) and FOSmix (60 vs 90%) was observed in the M2 medium.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.82 = 0.85 h. mu reported (calorimetric first fast phase, grouped substrates). Values are mmol/gDW accumulated per interval (0-24/24-48/48-168 h), not per-hour rates; totals=sum of intervals. Sign convention per paper footnote: negative AA/gas = production. Gas direction flagged uncertain (text says H2/CO2 produced during growth; late-phase negative values reported). Final pH 5.4+/-0.3 (M2), slightly lower than M20. Increased fructan consumption vs M20. FOSinu grew slowly (two-phase) in both media; long-chain inulin HP: no growth.
